instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `validate-range` that evaluates an expression and two bounds exactly once each; if the value is outside [min, max] inclusive, signal an error; otherwise return the value.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro validate-range (expr min max)
  (let ((g (gensym)) (gmin (gensym)) (gmax (gensym)))
    `(let ((,g ,expr) (,gmin ,min) (,gmax ,max))
       (unless (and (>= ,g ,gmin) (<= ,g ,gmax))
         (error "Value ~A out of range [~A, ~A]" ,g ,gmin ,gmax))
       ,g)))
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH

            (UNLESS (AND (>= #:G1 #:G2) (<= #:G1 #:G3))
              (ERROR "Value ~A out of range [~A, ~A]" #:G1 #:G2 #:G3))
            #:G1)

            (UNLESS (AND (>= #:G112 #:G113) (<= #:G112 #:G114))
